response.justification: Strong senior software engineer with 20 years of diverse technical experience and proven delivery across full-stack systems. However, lacks the director-level strategic credentials and established AI expertise required for a Principal Engineer role. Current AI involvement (2-3 months at re:cinq on MCP and agent infrastructure) is recent and implementation-focused rather than strategic. Missing preferred qualifications: no Master's/PhD in AI, no demonstrated AI products or platforms, and no evidence of directing AI strategy at organizational scale.
strengths: Extensive full-stack experience spanning 20 years across web, mobile, embedded systems, and infrastructure engineering with proven ability to ship complex products
Demonstrated technical mentorship and community leadership through founding GISCollective open-source platform, creating educational workshops, and organizing technical learning initiatives
Versatile polyglot engineer who learns new domains quickly and applies craftsmanship principles (TDD, CI/CD) consistently across diverse technology stacks
concerns: AI expertise gap: Only 2-3 months of hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, with no prior ML/AI background, educational foundation in AI (BS Computer Science predates modern AI era), or demonstrated AI products/platforms as required for the role

Missing strategic/director-level evidence: Resume shows strong individual contributor work but limited evidence of setting technical vision, influencing organizational strategy, or driving strategic partnerships—core responsibilities for a principal engineer role

Recent role start: Joined re:cinq in April 2025 (2 months ago); insufficient time to demonstrate impact or strategic thinking in current AI infrastructure work, suggesting candidacy premature for a director-level principal engineer position
